personality: 8 Hand-Drawn / Journal infused with warm saffron and gold accents
personality_one_liner: A tactile, grounded, and deeply calming spiritual space that feels like a sacred personal diary.

spacing.rule: Generous organic padding. Give components breathing room to maintain a calm, uncrowded layout.

do's: Use solid warm-tinted surfaces (surfaceSecondary, surfaceTertiary) to create depth instead of blur, matching the organic journal aesthetic.
dont's: Avoid heavy frosted glass or translucent overlays which conflict with the earthy, tactile theme.

screens.name: Jaap (Home)
screens.purpose: Core screen where the user selects a deity, taps the massive japa counter, and tracks their 108 mala progress.

states.empty: Prompt to 'Begin your daily chanting' with a warm hand-drawn illustration.
states.error: Failed to sync count. Retry button.

screens.layout: Top: Deity Selector (horizontal scroll of rounded avatar cards containing real images of deities with gradient-to-dark scrims). Center: Massive circular tap target taking up 40% of the screen height. Wrapped around it is an SVG circular progress ring tracking out of 108. The main button uses `brandPrimary`. Below: 'Streak' and 'Total Count' stats displayed in clean, large typography using the text font, anchored by subtle dividers.

screens.name: Tracker
screens.purpose: Brahmacharya/discipline daily check-in with a visual calendar heatmap.

states.empty: No tracking data yet. Large primary CTA 'Check in for today'.

screens.layout: Top: Large Fraunces headline for current streak count. Center: A GitHub-style calendar heatmap showing discipline consistency over the month (use small rounded squares colored from surfaceTertiary to brandPrimary based on intensity/success). Bottom: Month-at-a-glance stats (success days vs. relapses) in simple 2-column cards.

screens.name: Insights
screens.purpose: Analytics dashboard visualizing japa counts per deity and combined progress.

states.empty: Complete your first mala to see insights.

screens.layout: Top: Segmented pill control (All, Krishna, Shiva, Hanuman, etc.). Center: A clean bar chart or smooth line chart tracking daily count volumes. Keep axes minimal and rely on tooltips/labels. Bottom list: Milestone achievements (e.g., 100 malas) displayed as gold (brandSecondary) bordered cards with icons.

screens.name: Settings
screens.purpose: Manage app preferences like Language and Reminders.
screens.layout: Standard list view with tactile rounded list rows (surfaceSecondary). Include a bilingual toggle switch (Hindi/English) prominently at the top. Below: Daily reminder time picker and notification toggles.

instructions_to_main_agent: No indigo, violet, or blue colors allowed. Stick strictly to the warm saffron, temple gold, and soft cream palette.
Implement the 'Jaap' counter button as a massive, tactile circular area at the center of the screen, ensuring it is very easy to tap blindly without looking at the screen.
Wrap an SVG circular progress indicator around the main japa button using `react-native-svg` and `react-native-reanimated` to track the 108 counts of a mala smoothly.
Use Haptic feedback critically: `Medium` impact on every single tap of the counter, and `Success` notification feedback when a mala (108 count) is completed.
The Heatmap calendar should use grid views mapped to the user's data; color the days incrementally from `surfaceTertiary` (empty/0) up to `brandPrimary` (max intensity).
Use real images for the deities on the top horizontal selector list. Apply a gradient-to-dark scrim if you overlay text on these images.
Maintain the tactile 'journal' aesthetic. Avoid heavy glassmorphism/blurs and stick to solid surface offsets (e.g., surfaceSecondary on surface) with 0 shadow and subtle borders.
Add the text translation toggle functionality for Hindi/English right from the start in the Settings screen.
Include a 'Custom Mantra' option in the deity selector that uses the abstract temple image provided.
